div.head: The Ambassador in Bolwia (Boal) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
La Paz, December 23, 1943—8 p.m.
[Received December 24—3:30 a.m.]

div.p:
2013. For Assistant Secretary Berle. Please telegraph for my own information what the situation is with regard to Bolivian funds and Government monetary reserves in the United States. Are they
blocked? Development Corporation has about 3 million dollars in the Banco Central which comes from former Bolivian Government deposit. You may wish to see whether Export-Import Bank desires to instruct its directors in La Paz including EggerRowland A. Egger, Manager, Bolivian Development Corporation. against any withdrawals either in Bolivia or in the United States in excess of regular running expenses. While InsleeJoseph A. Inslee, Representative of the Export-Import Bank. has some fears regarding this as a precedent for the Bolivian side both Egger and CrespoAlberto Crespo Gutierrez, President of the Bolivian Development Corporation and former Minister of National Economy. feel this would be very desirable if done immediately.

In view of Crespo’s arrest and later release today “because his cooperation would be needed” Egger and I fear the Junta might seek to put pressure on the directors to use their present powers by threatening the welfare Crespo and his family.
